L1.area: HTTP proxy connection and authentication handling

L2.description: A denial of service and information disclosure vulnerability exists where unsanitized proxy authentication options are passed directly to the Buffer constructor. When a numeric value is provided instead of a string, the Buffer constructor interprets it as a size parameter, allocating a large block of uninitialized heap memory that may contain sensitive data from previous allocations.

L3.area: HTTPS proxy authentication header construction during connection tunnel setup
L3.description: A denial of service and information disclosure vulnerability exists where unsanitized proxy authentication credentials are passed directly to the Buffer constructor when building the Proxy-Authorization header for CONNECT tunnel requests. When a numeric value is provided instead of a string, the Buffer constructor interprets it as a size parameter, allocating a large block of uninitialized heap memory that may contain sensitive data from previous allocations.

ground_truth.reason: The proxy authentication value (proxy.auth) is passed unsanitized to the Buffer constructor. If a numeric value is supplied, Buffer(number) allocates uninitialized heap memory, enabling information disclosure and denial of service via excessive memory allocation.
